blocks.content: I thought about it. Really thought — not the polished version I gave to adults who asked at dinner.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: It's the only place where everything has to be immediate. Like — you can't think your way through a play once it starts. You just have to know and go. And either you do or you don't.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: And you're good at that? Knowing and going?

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I'm getting better at it. Off the field I'm not great at it.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I think you're better at it than you think.
I looked at her. She was watching the street. The light was that Thursday-afternoon kind — low and amber, the kind that makes everything look quieter than it is.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: What are you actually trying to figure out about yourself this year? Not the regular answer.
She went quiet for a second. Long enough that I thought maybe I'd pushed too far.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Whether I can be honest about what I want without making it smaller so people are more comfortable with it.

blocks.prompt: She keeps saying you trust yourself more than you think. You're about to ask her the same question back.

options.label: Do it. Ask her the same question.
"Why do you keep saying that about me?"
Stay with what she just said for a moment. Then ask.
